news.headline: US State Department: The US and Russia exchanged notes to finalize an understanding to ensure the stability of diplomatic banking for russian and US bilateral missions in meeting in Istanbul.
US State Department: The US and Russia exchanged notes to finalize an understanding to ensure the stability of diplomatic banking for Russian and US bilateral missions in a meeting in Istanbul.

news.summary: In Annapolis Junction, Maryland, Vibrint, a prominent player in federal technology solutions, has appointed Jim Robb as the new chief operating officer. This key move signifies the company's strategic direction and plans for growth in the market sector.
news.headline: Vibrint names Jim Robb as COO, leading federal tech solutions. #AI #COO #Vibrint

news.summary: Constellation Brands Inc (STZ) reported fourth-quarter earnings with net sales of $2.16 billion, surpassing estimates of $2.13 billion. Beer segment sales remained stable at $1.70 billion while Wine and Spirits sales rose 11% to $459.8 million. Beer shipment volume dropped by 1.8% while Wine and Spirits increased by 3.5%. Gross profit rose by 7% to $1.11 billion but the company reported an operating loss of $(150.3) million, a decline from $629.4 million the previous year. The adjusted EPS of $2.63 exceeded the estimated $2.28. Operating cash flow was $3.2 billion, a 13% Y/Y increase, with a free cash flow of $1.9 billion, a 28% Y/Y growth. The company had $68.1 million in cash by February 28 and 48 million hectoliters of production capacity in Mexico by the end of fiscal 2025. Additionally, Constellation declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.02 per share, payable on May 15.
news.headline: Constellation Brands reports Q4 earnings: net sales $2.16B, Beer sales $1.7B, Wine/Spirits $459.8M. Operating loss $(150.3)M. Adjusted EPS $2.63 beats $2.28 estimate. Cash flow: $3.2B increase YoY, $1.9B free cash flow YoY. 48M hectoliters production capacity in Mexico. Quarterly dividend: $1.02 per share, 1% increase.

news.summary: Investors who suffered losses in The Bancorp between January 25, 2024, and March 4, 2025, are encouraged by Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson to reach out directly to discuss their options. news.summary: IOG Resources II, LLC ("IOGR II") disclosed a new joint venture with Elevation Resources LLC, backed by Pine Brook Partners. The 2025 drilling initiative includes 10 horizontal Barnett wellbores situated in the Permian Basin in Andrews County, Texas. This collaboration marks the sixth investment in IOGR II and the eighteenth within the IOG Resources platform. Legal advice for IOGR II was provided by Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP.
news.headline: IOGR II partners with Elevation Resources in 10 wellbore project in Permian Basin. 6th investment for IOGR II, 18th for IOG Resources platform. Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP advises.

news.summary: On April 11th, Wells Fargo & Company (NYSE: WFC) will reveal their Q1 financial results before the market opens. Analysts predict earnings of $1.23 per share, an increase from $1.20 per share in the previous year. The company anticipates quarterly revenue of $20.76 billion, slightly lower than the $20.86 billion reported in the same period last year. Recently, Wells Fargo announced the conclusion of its 2021 consent order with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency regarding loss mitigation practices. Following this news, Wells Fargo's stock rose by 6.9% to close at $66.33 on Wednesday. For more details and analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.

news.summary: In a recent announcement on April 10, 2025, the Academy of Certified Portfolio Managers (ACPM) celebrated the graduation of the 2025 class of Certified Portfolio Managers (CPM). This prestigious designation provides financial professionals with advanced expertise in portfolio management. The newly certified CPMs are equipped with specialized skills and knowledge to excel in the field. This year, a significant number of individuals successfully completed the certification program, demonstrating their commitment to enhancing their portfolio management capabilities. The CPM® designation continues to uphold high standards in the industry, ensuring that professionals possess the necessary qualifications to navigate the complexities of managing portfolios effectively.

news.summary: Innoblative is gearing up to begin a feasibility study to assess the safety and efficacy of its SIRA® RFA Electrosurgical Device, which has been designated as an FDA Breakthrough Device. The study will focus on patients undergoing breast-conserving surgery. Breast cancer holds the top spot as the most frequently diagnosed cancer worldwide, affecting millions of individuals. The study will be conducted in Chicago and aims to gather crucial data on the device's performance in this clinical setting.
news.headline: Innoblative gets U.S. IDE for SIRA® RFA device for breast cancer, starting feasibility study. Breast cancer is top global cancer.

news.summary: A popular pet supplement brand in the UK, YuMOVE, recently transitioned its subscriber experience to Ordergroove's enterprise platform based in New York. By making this strategic change, YuMOVE anticipates an improvement in operational efficiency, paving the way for sustained and profitable growth. The brand found the need for a solution that could align with its expansion objectives and cater to the evolving needs of its customers, following various challenges encountered earlier.

news.summary: On Thursday, despite softer inflation and a temporary trade truce by President Trump, U.S. Treasury yields rose, indicating investor skepticism towards a Fed pivot and prolonged policy uncertainty. The 30-year Treasury note yield surged 8 basis points to 4.81%, recovering half of the previous day's decline due to the tariff pause. The 10-year yield increased by 3 basis points to 4.35%, despite positive macroeconomic signals. The iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (TLT) fell 1.5%. Inflation fell more than expected in March 2025, with CPI dropping to 2.4%, the lowest since September 2024, down from 2.8% in February. Monthly inflation also contracted by 0.1%, the first negative reading since May 2020. Bond investors are cautious, awaiting a clearer signal from the Fed.
news.headline: Treasury yields rose despite soft inflation data, with 30-year yield hitting 4.81% and 10-year yield at 4.35%. Market remains skeptical of Fed pivot. Inflation data shows CPI dropped more than expected, with annual rate at 2.4%, marking a significant decrease. Bond investors are waiting for clearer signal from the Fed amidst rising uncertainty.

news.summary: In a recent podcast, prominent technical analyst Kevin suggests that the current market turbulence may be signaling an imminent reversal following a predictable pattern. He noted that his three-month-old forecast predicted a correction lasting between 114 and 174 days, with the market now entering the critical "reversal zone." Kevin downplayed the panic caused by Trump's tariff announcements, labeling it as a media-driven emotional overreaction. He pointed out that every prediction he made is unfolding, indicating that tariff fears are subsiding and technical indicators align with historical precedents. The news that tariffs are paused has contributed to this sentiment.
news.headline: Bitcoin is back above $80,000 as technical analyst predicts upcoming reversal amid market turbulence following historical patterns.

Despite their efforts, there is doubt within the industry about the effectiveness of their opposition.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, in Reston, Virginia, Rafael Systems Global Sustainment (RSGS) announced the appointment of Jennifer Swanson to its Board of Directors. With more than 33 years of experience in government service, Jennifer brings expertise in defense procurement and digital strategies to the company. This addition strengthens the board with her valuable insights and leadership.

news.summary: CyberArk, a company based in New York, is collaborating with Accenture from Newton, Massachusetts to combine Accenture's AI Refinery™ with CyberArk's AI-enhanced Identity Security Platform for improved security measures. The partnership aims to leverage the expertise of the two companies to enhance the security features of CyberArk's platform. This integration signifies a strategic move by both companies to harness the power of artificial intelligence in strengthening identity security.
news.headline: CyberArk partners with Accenture to enhance identity security for AI agents using AI Refinery.

news.summary: Vortex Companies, a leading provider of trenchless infrastructure solutions, has recently announced its acquisition of an environmental services and trenchless solutions provider in the UK and Ireland. This move, completed by the 53-year-old company, aims to strengthen Vortex's presence in these regions. Widely recognized for its rapid growth, Vortex continues to expand its global reach. The acquisition is expected to have a significant impact on the company's operations in the UK and Ireland, consolidating its position as a key player in the industry.
news.headline: Vortex acquires McAllister Group to boost UK presence, expanding trenchless solutions; founded 53 years ago.

news.summary: In Denver on April 10, 2025, Ping Identity revealed their enhanced focus on supporting partners and customers through strategic investments. The company, renowned for safeguarding digital identities for major corporations globally, introduced a revamped approach highlighting partner collaborations. news.summary: A report on the global market for 3D Scanners has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings, indicating a projected increase in demand to US$8.8 billion by 2030. This is a significant rise from the estimated market size of US$4.9 billion in 2024. The compound annual growth rate (CAGR) for 3D Scanners is expected to be 10.2% between 2024 and 2030. The report provides an analysis of the market concerning scanner type, installation type, range, application, and end-use sector for various regional and country markets from 2021 to 2030.
news.headline: Global 3D Scanners market projected to reach $8.8 billion by 2030 from estimated $4.9 billion in 2024, growing at CAGR of 10.2%.

news.summary: An inventor from South River, New Jersey, introduced a new product called the E Z - P B & J on April 10, 2025. This innovative creation offers a convenient and mess-free way to enjoy peanut butter and jelly. The inventor aimed to streamline the process and eliminate the hassle of using jars and knives. The E Z - P B & J is designed to save time and enhance the experience of enjoying this classic combination. The product's release has garnered attention and is expected to revolutionize the way people consume PB & J.
news.headline: Inventor from South River, N.J. creates E Z - P B & J packaging for peanut butter & jelly, saves time & reduces mess.

news.summary: An optical switch startup called nEye Systems, established by UC Berkeley technologists, has recently wrapped up a Series B funding round amounting to $58 million. The leading investor in this round was Alphabet’s independent growth fund, CapitalG, joined by M12 - Microsoft’s venture fund, Micron Ventures, NVIDIA, and Socratic Partners. With this recent investment, nEye has accumulated a total funding of $72.5 million, combining this Series B with an earlier undisclosed Series A that was helmed by TEDA Holdings.
news.headline: nEye Systems secures $72.5M to innovate AI infrastructure using optical switch tech. $58M in Series B led by CapitalG.

news.summary: In Los Angeles, HopSkipDrive, a tech company addressing transportation issues with a focus on safety, access, and care, revealed the addition of Dave Lissy to its Board of Directors. Lissy, previously the CEO of Bright Horizons Family Solutions (NYSE: BFAM) for 16 years until 2018, brings a wealth of experience in education and care to the role. Under his tenure, Bright Horizons solidified its position as a top provider of early education services.
news.headline: HopSkipDrive appoints Dave Lissy, former CEO of Bright Horizons, to its Board of Directors for transportation safety.

news.summary: In Arlington, Virginia, the prominent health intelligence network, Surescripts®, revealed that its technology and data infrastructure systems have received certified status from HITRUST for information security. The HITRUST r2 Certification highlights that Surescripts' systems have successfully met rigorous regulatory compliance and industry-specific standards, ensuring effective risk management. This recognition positions Surescripts as a leader in safeguarding health information.
news.headline: Surescripts earns HITRUST r2 Certification for information security compliance, ensuring highest level of data protection.Important numbers: - HITRUST r2 Certification achieved- Surescripts is the nation's leading health intelligence network

news.summary: A company based in Schaumburg, Illinois known as Analog2Fidelity has made headlines by reviving the Koetsu phono cartridge line with the help of the original craftsmen. The company recently revealed plans for the global premiere of three Koetsu cartridges at AXPONA 2025.
